goal: Optimize homepage SEO and AI-search meta tags

summary.title: Optimize homepage SEO and AI-search meta tags
summary.what: Added layout-level SEO meta support for keywords, robots, author, format-detection, Twitter account tags, locale-aware Open Graph locale tags, and per-page social title/description overrides. Updated homepage meta copy to fit the requested character ranges and added WebSite JSON-LD to the homepage schema set.
summary.why: The user provided an SEO audit for mainline.sh showing missing basic technical meta tags and social sharing tags, plus overlong homepage description and Open Graph copy.
summary.user_goal: Make mainline.sh friendlier to Google, Bing, Perplexity, ChatGPT, Gemini, and social crawlers before launch.

decisions.chose: Keep the normal HTML title keyword-rich while using separate shorter Open Graph and Twitter titles.
decisions.rationale: Search result titles, Open Graph previews, and Twitter cards have different length targets, so the layout now supports separate values instead of reusing pageTitle everywhere.

decisions.chose: Add default metadata at BaseLayout level.
decisions.rationale: Every page now gets keywords, robots, author, format-detection, twitter:site, and twitter:creator unless a page overrides them.

decisions.chose: Use WebSite JSON-LD alongside existing Organization, SoftwareApplication, and WebPage schema.
decisions.rationale: This gives AI/search crawlers a clear site-level entity for Mainline and its category terms without changing visible UI.

decisions.chose: Do not add FAQPage JSON-LD in this pass.
decisions.rationale: FAQ structured data should correspond to visible FAQ content; adding invisible Q&A markup would be brittle and less trustworthy.

rejected.reason: The audit applies different length windows to each surface, so separate fields are cleaner and more controllable.
The goal is machine-readable clarity, not low-value keyword stuffing.

risks: twitter:site and twitter:creator currently use @mainline_sh; update BaseLayout if the official X handle is different.

acknowledged_constraints.note: This work did not claim that mainline init writes AGENTS.md.
Chinese and Spanish social meta copy was added; the existing native-speaker review concern remains open.
Localized copy remains launch-sensitive and should still be reviewed by native speakers.

review_notes: Homepage meta description is now 140 characters.
Homepage og:title is 28 characters and og:description is 57 characters.
Generated HTML includes keywords, robots, author, format-detection, twitter:site, twitter:creator, and WebSite JSON-LD.
Ran npm run build with 0 Astro check errors.
Production deployment dpl_DgNvGWegwx9pAgj6fMLV5HHJBFQb completed and is aliased to https://mainline.sh.
Verified the live homepage HTML contains the updated meta tags.
Ran npm run indexnow after deployment; 64 URLs were submitted with HTTP status 200.

architectural_claims: BaseLayout now owns default SEO and social metadata for all pages.
Pages can override Open Graph and Twitter title/description independently from the HTML title and meta description.
The homepage emits Organization, WebSite, SoftwareApplication, and WebPage JSON-LD.
behavioral_changes: The homepage description now fits the requested 120-160 character range.
The homepage Open Graph title and description now use shorter social-specific copy.
The homepage Twitter title and description now use Twitter-specific copy.
All pages rendered through BaseLayout include keywords, robots, author, format-detection, twitter:site, and twitter:creator meta tags.
Open Graph locale metadata is emitted for English, Chinese, and Spanish variants.
security_implications: No user data, auth, cookies, scripts, forms, or tracking behavior changed.
migration_notes: Future pages can pass ogTitle, ogDescription, twitterTitle, and twitterDescription to BaseLayout when audit-specific social copy is needed.
If the official X handle changes, update the default twitterSite and twitterCreator values in BaseLayout.
